lessons.title: Learn: File System Exploration
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 03: File System Exploration

In this lab, you will learn how to explore Linux directories, inspect file metadata, and locate files quickly.

## Learning Objectives

- Navigate deep directory paths confidently
- Use listing and inspection commands (`ls`, `stat`, `file`)
- Understand hidden files and directory structure
- Search files and directories with `find`
- Use `du` to inspect folder size usage

## Study Topics

### 1) Exploring Directory Trees

```bash
pwd
ls
ls -la
cd /etc
cd ~
```

### 2) Hidden Files and Metadata

```bash
ls -la ~
stat ~/.bashrc
file /etc/passwd
```

### 3) Finding Files

```bash
find ~ -name '*.txt'
find /etc -name 'passwd'
```

### 4) Measuring Directory Size

```bash
du -sh ~
du -sh ~/lab3
```

### 5) Safe Exploration Rules

- Use read-only commands while exploring (`ls`, `cat`, `find`, `du`)
- Avoid deleting files during discovery tasks
- Use absolute paths when unsure (`/home/hacker/...`)

After reading, continue to command practice and complete exercises before the MCQ exam.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Run these commands in sequence to build exploration habits.

```bash
cd ~ && pwd
mkdir -p ~/lab3/projects ~/lab3/logs ~/lab3/archive
ls ~/lab3
ls -la ~/lab3
touch ~/lab3/projects/app.txt
echo 'project alpha' > ~/lab3/projects/app.txt
cat ~/lab3/projects/app.txt
stat ~/lab3/projects/app.txt
file ~/lab3/projects/app.txt
find ~/lab3 -name 'app.txt'
```

tasks.description: C01: Move to home directory and print location.
C02: Create lab3 with subfolders projects, logs, archive.
C03: List contents of lab3.
C04: List all files in lab3 including hidden metadata.
C05: Create file app.txt inside projects.
C06: Write project alpha into app.txt.
C07: Display file content from app.txt.
C08: Show detailed metadata for app.txt.
C09: Identify file type of app.txt.
C10: Find app.txt inside lab3.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Continue with advanced exploration and filtering.

```bash
find ~/lab3 -type d
find ~/lab3 -type f
printf 'log1
log2
log3
' > ~/lab3/logs/system.log
head -n 2 ~/lab3/logs/system.log
tail -n 1 ~/lab3/logs/system.log
wc -l ~/lab3/logs/system.log
ls -R ~/lab3
du -sh ~/lab3
grep 'log2' ~/lab3/logs/system.log
find ~/lab3 -name '*.log'
```

tasks.description: C11: List directories only inside lab3.
C12: List files only inside lab3.
C13: Create system.log with 3 lines.
C14: Show first two lines of system.log.
C15: Show last line of system.log.
C16: Count lines in system.log.
C17: Recursively list all files under lab3.
C18: Show summary size of lab3.
C19: Search for log2 in system.log.
C20: Find all .log files in lab3.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Apply your exploration skills to complete all 5 tasks.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Create ~/lab3/docs and add readme.txt with text 'filesystem map'.
E2: Find readme.txt from home directory.
E3: Show detailed metadata for readme.txt.
E4: Count words in readme.txt.
E5: Find all txt files inside lab3 and confirm readme.txt is present.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: File System Exploration

Answer with option index (0-3). Example: submit `2`.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command shows file metadata like size and modify time?
Q2: Which command identifies the type of a file?
Q3: Which command finds files by name recursively?
Q4: What does `du -sh folder` show?
Q5: Which command recursively lists files and subdirectories?
